meta.title: Blue Prince — Room & Item Log
meta.note: Seed/example data. Entries are edited via a Claude chat and committed to main; the viewer reads this file live via the GitHub raw URL. Replace the examples below with your own findings.

runs.notes: First logged run. 'rank' = furthest rank/row reached (the house has 9; the Antechamber is rank 9).

rooms.name: Entrance Hall
rooms.effect: The fixed starting room. Every day begins here; if you have any Allowance, a coin packet appears here each morning.

rooms.name: Hallway
rooms.effect: Connector room. Cheap to draft and helps link adjoining rooms; influences what you draw from it.

rooms.name: Bedroom
rooms.effect: Bedrooms belong to the Sinclair family and staff and tend to grant extra Steps.

rooms.name: Courtyard
rooms.effect: Outdoor/garden room. Green rooms usually contain a Dig Spot that rewards Gems fairly often.

rooms.name: Laboratory
rooms.effect: Grants a special ability when placed. Like other ability/mechanic rooms, it costs at least one Gem to draft.

rooms.name: Boiler Room
rooms.effect: Red room tied to the house's utility mechanics. Red rooms are usually detrimental — often a dead end or carrying a negative effect.

rooms.name: Spare Room
rooms.effect: A blank room that can be permanently upgraded by Upgrade Disks. It can be upgraded twice — first into one of three room types, then with a type-specific effect (12 possible upgrades in total).

variants.name: Spare Bedroom
variants.effect: Bedroom-type upgrade. Sub-upgrades mimic Servant's Quarters, Her Ladyship's Chamber, or the Master Bedroom; effects match the originals.
variants.notes: Each sub-upgrade has its own item list, based on (but not identical to) the room it mimics.

variants.name: Spare Hall
variants.effect: Hall-type upgrade. Sub-upgrades mimic the Foyer, the Secret Passage, or the Great Hall.

variants.name: Spare Greenroom
variants.effect: Green-room-type upgrade. Sub-upgrades mimic the Veranda, the Terrace, or the Patio.

rooms.notes: Example of a room with variants — upgraded floorplans keep this room's directory slot but can change color and effect.

items.name: Key
items.effect: Used to unlock locked doors. The most common of the three main currencies.
items.location: Found around the house, in chests, and bought from shops.

items.name: Gem
items.effect: Primary currency for drafting certain floorplans (ability/mechanic rooms). Comes in six cosmetic colors with no gameplay difference.
items.location: Dig Spots in green rooms, chests, and shops. Rarest of the three main currencies.

items.name: Coin (Gold)
items.effect: Used to purchase items and services from Shops. Found in piles of up to five, collected in a single action.
items.location: Around the house; an Allowance packet also appears in the Entrance Hall each morning.

items.name: Ivory Die
items.effect: Spent during drafting to redraw the offered floorplans.
items.location: Found in the house and obtained from certain rooms.

items.name: Upgrade Disk
items.effect: Permanently adds an upgrade to a floorplan (e.g. turns a Spare Room into one of its variants). The upgrade sticks to that floorplan across runs.
items.location: Data disks found around the estate.

sightings.notes: Example sighting: in run 1, the Entrance Hall contained a coin packet.
Example sighting: in run 1, the Courtyard's dig spot yielded a gem.

puzzles.name: Example Puzzle
puzzles.location: Room or area where the puzzle appears
puzzles.solution: The full answer goes here — this log stores complete solutions/spoilers.
puzzles.notes: Example entry showing the puzzle shape. status is 'open' or 'solved'.

notes.text: Log created (schema v2: runs + per-run sightings + room variants). Replace the example entries above with real findings.
